top_categories.workout_context: 200 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 200 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
At Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 200 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
19,700ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
19,700ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
98ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
200 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 19,700ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 200 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 200 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive with 19,700ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mixed terrain at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 200 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 200 miles of mixed terrain in Exeter Racecourse, Kennford, Exeter, Devon, EX6 7XS, England.
